description:
Replace `fz_alloc_frame(cont.fn_id.0, ...)` with `fz_alloc_frame(resolved_sid, schemas[sid].size)` at every emit-site that materializes a continuation frame.

## What

(a) Add helper `compute_cont_slot0_descr(terminator, fn_types, module) -> Descr` that mirrors `src/ir_typer.rs:190-215` exactly:
  - `Term::Call { callee, args }`: resolve the callee's specialized return by joining over its `Return` terminators under that callsite's `FnTypes`.
  - `Term::CallClosure` / `Term::Receive`: `Descr::any()`.

(b) At every cont-frame alloc site in `src/ir_codegen.rs`, build the lookup key as `[slot0_descr, ...captured_descrs]` and resolve via `spec_registry.resolve(cont.fn_id, &key)`. Panic loudly on miss (same .29.11 discipline as `resolve_callee_sid`).

(c) Use resolved SpecId for both `fz_alloc_frame` (size lookup) AND for the schema_id field written into the frame header.

## Why

Trampoline already dispatches by `header.schema_id` indexing `fn_ptrs`. Today indirect-dispatch hardcodes `cont.fn_id.0` (the any-key SpecId by invariant). Switching to a resolved SpecId is the prerequisite for typed dispatch on continuations.

## Tests (TDD)

1. Unit test for `compute_cont_slot0_descr`: small IR fixture, assert the helper output matches the typer's `callsite_keys[cont.fn_id]` slot 0 for each terminator kind.
2. Integration: a program with a Cont whose typer-registered narrow spec differs from its any-key spec. Verify (a) trampoline dispatches to the narrow body's `fn_ptrs[sid]`, (b) parity across interp/JIT/AOT.

## Invariants preserved

- Any-key still registered for every fn pre-.29.12.6, so subsumption resolve is the safety net if slot-0 logic drifts.
- One ticket == one commit. No warnings, all tests pass.
title: Cont-frame alloc uses resolved SpecId
